behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between request-construction logic and the API client call payload (MOCK-MISS: cross-module wiring can drop/reshape fields). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch it because a fake client can capture the outgoing payload (REAL-INFRA: no). Correctness does not depend on model judgment/output quality (LLM-DEP: no), and there is no multi-agent sequence (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about request payload completeness, not live provider behavior or model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stubbed API client that records the dispatched request. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert the sent payload contains the full taxonomy label set (exact members, no omissions) on every call path.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization/validation logic at a single module boundary and can be triggered with a fixed raw label fixture (MOCK-MISS: no required inter-module failure). No real infra needed (REAL-INFRA: no). No dependence on real model output quality because assertion is over a supplied invalid label string (LLM-DEP: no). No chained agentic steps (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on mapping unexpected label to "unknown", which is local post-processing.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/classification-result mapping function with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw provider label like "medical"; assert returned label is exactly "unknown" and never the raw value.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets determinism of the normalization step itself, which is pure/local and reproducible with identical fixtures (MOCK-MISS: no). No OS/network/subprocess needed (REAL-INFRA: no). No real LLM dependency because inputs are fixed raw responses (LLM-DEP: no). No multi-step agent pipeline (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text says "identical raw API responses," disqualifying need for live model variability checks.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-test) with the same raw API response payload and taxonomy; assert identical final label across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency tied to provider response evolution, so correctness depends on real model/provider outputs in execution (LLM-DEP: yes). Mocking would hide true variability and provider-side changes (MOCK-HIDE: yes). This is not a multi-agent pipeline; it is repeated single-step classification calls (MULTI-STEP: no), so Workflow is not required. Rejected lower levels: Integration with stubs cannot reveal real provider/model consistency characteristics stated as "live API calls."
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same input/taxonomy against the provider, capturing raw provider responses and final mapped labels. Assert final labels are stable whenever raw provider label/decision is unchanged; allow drift only when provider response content differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is ordering/handoff across components: budget checker + request builder + dispatcher (MOCK-MISS: yes, can fail when modules interact even if each unit passes). Real OS/network is unnecessary; a fake client can verify dispatch/no-dispatch and payload size after truncation (REAL-INFRA: no). No dependence on model output quality (LLM-DEP: no). No multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, wire module with a stub API client, and submit an over-budget document. Assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs before any external dispatch, and any dispatched request respects the configured token ceiling.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on provider-reported usage from a real AI API call; a stubbed usage field could always pass and hide real tokenizer/model-side accounting differences (MOCK-HIDE: yes). Real external call is required, and this path goes through an AI/ML API (LLM-DEP default applies), so minimum is Agentic rather than Integration. Not a multi-step agent pipeline (MULTI-STEP: no), so Workflow not required. Rejected lower levels: behavior text requires "provider-reported" usage, which disqualifies purely local stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live calls for representative "typical" documents using configured model/settings; record provider usage metadata for each call and assert reported prompt/total tokens are <= configured per-call budget under the module’s enforcement policy.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration across API client response handling + backoff/timer + retry loop (MOCK-MISS: yes at module interaction boundary). In-process fakes can deterministically simulate rate-limit signals and verify wait/retry behavior, so real infra is not strictly required (REAL-INFRA: no). Correctness does not depend on model output content, only transport/error semantics (LLM-DEP: no). No multi-agent sequence (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about handling rate-limit signal and retry policy, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub client that returns a rate-limit response (e.g., 429/too-many-requests) then success; run classifier with controllable clock/sleeper and assert it waits per policy, retries, and returns successful classification instead of surfacing immediate error.
